perturbation.original_text: 1.52 "Long-Acting β Adrenoceptor Agonist" or "LABA" means a chemical entity that (i) selectively binds to human β adrenoceptors and activates human β adrenoceptors at concentrations less than 100 nanomolar and (ii) has significantly longer activity than salmeterol after inhalation dosing as determined in a guinea pig acetylcholine bronchoprotection model or similar animal model.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.52 "Long-Acting β Adrenoceptor Agonist" or "LABA" means a chemical entity that (i) selectively binds to human β adrenoceptors and activates human β adrenoceptors.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition of "Long-Acting β Adrenoceptor Agonist" or "LABA" removes the criteria for measuring the duration of activity compared to salmeterol. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because later sections of the contract refer to the need for demonstrating a significantly longer duration of activity (as seen in Schedule 1.19 criteria for new compounds), but the primary definition no longer includes this as a necessary characteristic of a LABA. This contradiction introduces ambiguity in determining which compounds qualify as LABAs under the agreement, affecting the scope of the collaboration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schedule 1.19 Criteria for Theravance New Compounds and Replacement Compounds 7. Duration of agonist activity in vivo to be clearly longer than that of salmeterol. This would be at least 72 hours in the Theravance model. The exact duration criterion for either the GSK or the Theravance model might be modified in the light of forthcoming clinical data from the program.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of LABA removes the requirement for the compound to have a significantly longer activity than salmeterol. This directly contradicts Schedule 1.19, which explicitly requires 'Duration of agonist activity in vivo to be clearly longer than that of salmeterol.' By removing the duration requirement from the definition, the modified text creates a conflict with the criteria outlined in Schedule 1.19, thus introducing ambiguity and a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2.2 GSK Funding Responsibility. GSK shall bear all costs and expenses associated with the Development of Collaboration Products for Commercialization including those incurred by Theravance (or to which it has become obligated) after the signature date of this Agreement and which previously have been discussed with and agreed to by GSK and, so far as the aforementioned Theravance costs are concerned, for the avoidance of doubt, the maximum amount shall not exceed U.S. $2,940,000.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2.2 GSK Funding Responsibility. GSK shall bear all costs and expenses associated with the Development of Collaboration Products for Commercialization including those incurred by Theravance (or to which it has become obligated) after the signature date of this Agreement and which previously have been discussed with and agreed to by GSK and, so far as the aforementioned Theravance costs are concerned, for the avoidance of doubt, the maximum amount shall not exceed U.S. $1,000,000.
perturbation.explanation: By reducing the maximum funding amount from U.S. $2,940,000 to U.S. $1,000,000, this perturbation creates a conflict. The original agreement states that GSK will cover Theravance's development costs within the higher limit. The modified version limits this funding, which creates uncertainty about which costs exceeding U.S. $1,000,000 will be covered. This contradiction affects Section 6.1.4, which anticipates milestone payments to Theravance based on the successful progression of these compounds. If Theravance's development is hindered by insufficient funding, the milestone payments could be jeopardized, creating a financial in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1.4 One-Time Fee for Each Theravance New Compound. Within thirty days following the acceptance by the Joint Project Committee or the Joint Steering Committee of each of the two Theravance New Compounds to be contributed to the collaboration pursuant to Section 4.1, and in further partial consideration for the acquisition of license rights under the Theravance Patents and the Theravance Know-How by GSK under this Agreement, GSK shall pay to Theravance a non-creditable, non-refundable amount of Five Million United States Dollars (U.S.$5,000,000) for each such Theravance New Compound.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the maximum funding available to Theravance for development costs from $2,940,000 to $1,000,000. This contradicts Section 6.1.4 because it introduces uncertainty about whether Theravance will have sufficient funding to successfully develop the 'Theravance New Compounds'. If the development of these compounds is hindered by the reduced funding, GSK's obligation to pay the $5,000,000 milestone payment outlined in Section 6.1.4 becomes uncertain. Therefore, the reduced funding limit directly contradicts the expectation that GSK will pay the milestone payments upon successful development of the compounds.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1.2 Stock Purchase. On the Effective Date, GSK will purchase 4,000,000 shares of Theravance Series E Preferred Stock at a price of U.S.$10.00 per share for total consideration of Forty Million United States Dollars (U.S. $40,000,000). Such purchase will be made pursuant to the Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement attached hereto as Schedule 6.1.2.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1.2 Stock Purchase. On the Effective Date, GSK will purchase 2,000,000 shares of Theravance Series E Preferred Stock at a price of U.S.$10.00 per share for total consideration of Twenty Million United States Dollars (U.S. $20,000,000). Such purchase will be made pursuant to a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the stock purchase from 4,000,000 to 2,000,000 shares for a total of $20,000,000 contradicts the original financial commitments outlined in the agreement. This change directly impacts the capital Theravance receives, which affects its ability to fulfill its obligations under the agreement, as stated in Sections 4.1 (pooling of compounds). By changing it to a 'separate agreement' creates an in-text contradiction, where the original agreement is reffering to an amount of stack purchase and reffering to a schedule of the purchase. By making a separate agreement that isn't Schedule 6.1.2 it creates an in-text contradiction where no schedule exists for the newly created stock purchase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1. Pooling of Compounds. Subject to and consistent with the further Development principles outlined herein, each Party will offer a minimum of four (4) identified LABA compounds to this collaboration, with the intention of commercializing at least one Long-Acting β2 Adrenoceptor Agonist as a single agent and/or as a LABA/ICS Combination Product. Upon commencement of the collaboration pursuant to this Agreement, GSK and Theravance will contribute the following LABA compounds as Pooled Compounds to the collaboration: GSK Compounds GW 597901, GW 678007, GW 642444 and GW 774419 and Theravance Compounds TD-3327 and AMI-15471. For the avoidance of doubt, it is agreed and hereby acknowledged by both Parties that the compounds GW 597901, GW 678007, GW 642444 and GW 774419, TD-3327 and AMI-15471 are hereby accepted as Pooled Compounds. Theravance will provide two (2) Theravance New Compounds to the collaboration within eighteen (18) months of the Effective Date in order to meet the requirement that Theravance contribute a total of four (4) LABA compounds to the Pooled Compounds. Without prejudice to the foregoing, GSK will endeavor to provide Theravance, upon Theravance's request and at GSK's expense and discretion, such assistance as may be reasonably required by Theravance to achieve this objective, including providing directly or through GSK's vendors, assistance in (i) chemical process development, (ii) salt selection, (iii) pharmaceutical formulation, (iv) toxicological evaluation, and (v) API preparation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the stock purchase amount and specifies that the purchase will be made pursuant to a 'separate agreement' instead of the original Schedule 6.1.2. This directly contradicts the initial commitment and the previously established financial structure within the original agreement, including how that investment by GSK would allow Theravance to perform its obligations such as the pooling of compounds described in Section 4.1. The reduction of funds could impair Theravance's ability to provide the agreed-upon compounds, creating an in-text contradiction.
